{
  "claims": [
    "The Renter’s Tax Credit in California has not been increased in over four decades.",
    "The average home price in California increased from $85,000 in 1979 to over $900,000 today.",
    "The current $7,000 Homeowner’s Exemption saves homeowners just $70 annually on property taxes.",
    "The Renter’s Tax Credit offers only $60 for single filers and $120 for joint filers.",
    "Senator Grove’s legislation aimed to increase the Homeowner’s Exemption from $7,000 to $50,000.",
    "Senator Grove’s legislation would have increased the Renter’s Tax Credit from $60 to $275 for single individuals and from $120 to $550 for joint filers.",
    "The proposed increases in the Homeowner's Exemption and Renter's Tax Credit reflect inflation adjustments based on costs originating from 1972."
  ]
}
